danger_signals.visual_form: Aliens bursting out of plants, mailboxes, trash cans, drums, and other street furniture
danger_signals.meaning: Threat is pervasive and hidden; danger can manifest anywhere in the environment

danger_signals.visual_form: Alien boss entity with three distinct forms throughout combat
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat intensity; enemy adaptability signals increasing danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Skull projectiles that appear after aliens are killed
danger_signals.meaning: Transitional danger state; opportunity window for resource collection before next threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Final mission stage set inside alien spacecraft
danger_signals.meaning: Highest threat level; player is now in enemy territory at source of invasion

safety_signals.visual_form: Flying skull pickups that restore life and energy reserves
safety_signals.meaning: Defeated enemies provide resources; progression is rewarded

safety_signals.visual_form: Stage transitions between side-scrolling combat, shooting gallery, and horizontal shooter sections
safety_signals.meaning: Successful completion of objectives advances world state and mission progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Three distinct playable characters with different visual designs and weapon types
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and ability to prepare; choice reflects tactical readiness

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Team designation as Earth's defense force
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Earth locations (streets, urban environments) as primary territory; progress toward alien mothership as invasion countermeasure

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple alien creature types; boss forms with three distinct configurations; aliens capable of hiding and emerging from environment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: UFO structures; alien mothership; infected/occupied civilian objects (plants, mailboxes); final stage is interior of mothership
Urban streets and civilian infrastructure as battlegrounds; mission objectives involve rescuing people

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Urban environments with street-level alien combat; civilians present as rescue objectives
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Aliens hide in everyday objects; infiltration tactics suggest occupation of human spaces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Street environments with destructible objects and alien emergence points; UFO targets visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Direct alien combat with escalating difficulty; multiple alien types coordinate assault

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition to shooting gallery and horizontal shooter perspectives; enemy bases as final objectives
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gameplay shifts from melee-focused to projectile-focused; aliens defend structured positions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Interior of alien mothership as final stage; alien brain/mothership boss as ultimate threat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss exhibits multiple forms; highest difficulty spike; destruction of mothership core is end-game condition

environmental_storytelling.example: Aliens conceal themselves in plants, mailboxes, trash cans, and drums—everyday urban objects become danger sources, communicating invasion permeates civilian life
Missions progress from city streets to UFO targets to final mothership interior—spatial progression mirrors strategic escalation from defense to offense
Each mission has distinct goals (rescue people, destroy UFO)—environmental context changes signal different aspects of the invasion crisis
Single boss with three distinct forms appears mid-game, then repeats as common enemy near final mission—suggests alien adaptation or mass production of dangerous variants
Transition from side-scrolling to shooting gallery to horizontal shooter formats—environmental changes reinforce tactical variety and threat diversity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Emerge from hidden positions in environmental objects; engage in direct combat with player
Changes form during combat (three distinct configurations); later appears as repeated common enemy, suggesting replication
Appear after alien defeat; can be shot for resource collection; indicate vulnerability window
Garth summons U.S. Air Force strike; Karen calls nuclear missile; Scooter deploys teleport-bomb trap—behavior reflects individual tactical specialization and resource cost
Serves as final stage environment; contains alien brain boss; requires infiltration and destruction to end invasion

notes: Alien Storm communicates world-state primarily through environmental design and gameplay perspective shifts rather than explicit visual markers. The game uses location progression (streets → UFOs → mothership) to signal invasion escalation. Threat communication relies on hidden emergence mechanics (aliens in objects) to convey infiltration and pervasiveness. Faction distinction is implicit: humans occupy streets/civilization, aliens invade from above and hide within. The three-character roster with distinct special attacks (military strike, nuclear response, tech-based trap) suggests different human defensive strategies. Boss morphology and eventual mass appearance implies alien evolutionary capability or rapid reproduction—a visual-behavioral narrative of threat escalation.
